content: The feasible region for the constraints $x-y \geq 0, x-5 y \leq-5, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$ is shown by the figure:

A manufacturing company produces two items, A and B. Each toy should be processed by two machines, I and II. Machine I can be operated for maximum 10 hours 40 minutes. It takes 20 minutes for an item of A and 15 minutes for B. Machine II can be operated for a total time at 8 hours 20 minutes. It takes 5 minutes for an item A and 8 minutes for B . The profit per item of $A$ is $Rs 25$ and per item of $B$ is ₹ 18 . The formulation of an L.P.P. to maximize the profit (where $x$ is number of items A and $y$ is the number of item $B$ ) is

options.content:
Maximize $z=25 x+18 y$

$$ \begin{aligned} \text { subject to } 20 x+15 y & \leq 640 \\ 5 x+8 y & \leq 500 \\ x, y & \geq 0 \end{aligned} $$
$$ \begin{array}{r} \text { Maximize } z=25 x+18 y \\ \text { subject to } 20 x+5 y \leq 8 \\ 5 x+8 y \leq 10 \\ x, y \geq 0 \end{array} $$
$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { Maximize } \mathrm{z}=25 x+18 y \\ & \text { subject to } 4 x+3 y \leq 128 \\ & \qquad \begin{array}{r} 5 x+8 y \geq 500 \\ x, y \geq 0 \end{array} \end{aligned} $$

content: The solution for minimizing the function $\mathrm{z}=x+y$ under an L.P.P. with constraints $x+y \geq 2, x+2 y \leq 8, y \leq 3, x, y \geq 0$ is

options.content: at infinite number of points but bounded set

explanation:
The corner points of the feasible region are

$\mathrm{A}(2,0), \mathrm{B}(8,0), \mathrm{C}(2,3), \mathrm{D}(0,3)$ and $\mathrm{E}(0,2)$.

At A $(2,0), \mathrm{z}=2+0=2$

At $\mathrm{B}(8,0), \mathrm{z}=8+0=8$

At $\mathrm{C}(2,3), \mathrm{z}=2+3=5$

At $\mathrm{D}(0,3), \mathrm{z}=0+3=3$

At $\mathrm{E}(0,2), \mathrm{z}=0+2=2$

$\therefore \quad \mathrm{z}$ has minimum values at $\mathrm{A}(2,0)$ and $\mathrm{E}(0,2)$

$\therefore \quad z$ has infinite solutions on seg AE.

content: In L.P.P., the maximum value of objective function $\mathrm{Z}=6 x+3 y$ subject to constraints $x+y \leq 5, x+2 y \geq 4,4 x+y \leq 12, x, y \geq 0$ is
explanation:
The feasible region lies on the origin side of $x+y=5$ and $4 x+y=12$, on the non-origin side of $x+2 y=4$, and it is in the first quadrant.

The corner points of the feasible region are

$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A}(0,2), \mathrm{B}\left(\frac{20}{7}, \frac{4}{7}\right), \mathrm{C}\left(\frac{7}{3}, \frac{8}{3}\right) \text { and } \mathrm{D}(0,5) \\ & \text { At } \mathrm{A}(0,2), \mathrm{Z}=6(0)+3(2)=6 \\ & \text { At } \mathrm{B}\left(\frac{20}{7}, \frac{4}{7}\right), \mathrm{Z}=6\left(\frac{20}{7}\right)+3\left(\frac{4}{7}\right)=\frac{132}{7} \\ & \text { At } \mathrm{C}\left(\frac{7}{3}, \frac{8}{3}\right), \mathrm{Z}=6\left(\frac{7}{3}\right)+3\left(\frac{8}{3}\right)=22 \\ & \text { At } \mathrm{D}(0,5)=6(0)+3(5)=15 \end{aligned} $$

$\therefore \quad$ Maximum value of Z is 22 .

content: The solution set of the constraints $|x-y| \leq 1, x, y \geq 0$ is

options.content: such that feasible region does not exist

explanation:
The feasible region lies on the origin side of $|x-y|=1$ and is in the first quadrant.

$\therefore \quad$ The common region is unbounded.

content: The difference between the maximum value and minimum value of objective function $\mathrm{z}=3 x+5 y$ subject to constraints $x+3 y \leq 60$, $x+y \geq 10, x-y \geq 0, x, y \geq 0$ is
explanation:
The corner points of the feasible region region are

$$ A(0,10), B(5,5), C(15,15) \text { and } D(0,20) $$

At A $(0,10), \mathrm{z}=50$

At $\mathrm{B}(5,5), \mathrm{z}=40$

At C $(15,15), \mathrm{z}=120$

At $\mathrm{D}(0,20), \mathrm{z}=100$

$$ \begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \mathrm{z}_{\max }-\mathrm{z}_{\min } & =120-40 \\ & =80 \end{aligned} $$

$\quad y-x \leqslant 1,2 x+5 y \leqslant 10, x+y \geqslant 1, x, y \geqslant 0$
$\quad y-x \geqslant 1,2 x+5 y \leqslant 10, x+y \geqslant 1, x, y \geqslant 0$
$\quad x-y \leqslant 1,2 x+5 y \geqslant 10, x+y \leqslant 1, x, y \geqslant 0$

explanation:
Here,

$\mathrm{L}_1: y-x=1$

$\mathrm{L}_2: 2 x+5 y=10$

$\mathrm{L}_3: x+y=1$

Feasible region lies on non-origin side of $\mathrm{L}_1$ and $L_3$ and on origin side of $L_2$.

content: If the difference between the maximum and minimum values of the objective function $\mathrm{z}=7 x-8 y$, subject to the constraints $x+y \leqslant 20, y \geqslant 5, x, y \geqslant 0$ is $5 \mathrm{k}+200$, then the value of k is
explanation:
The feasible region lies on the origin side of $x+y=20$, on the non-origin side of $y=5$, and it is in the first quadrant.

The corner points of the feasible region are, A $(0,20)$, B $(0,5)$ and C $(15,5)$

At A $(0,20), \mathrm{z}=7(0)-8(20)=-160$

At $\mathrm{B}(0,5), \mathrm{z}=7(0)-8(5)=-40$

At $\mathrm{C}(15,5), \mathrm{z}=7(15)-8(5)=65$

$\Rightarrow$ Maximum value of $z=65$

Minimum value of $\mathrm{z}=-160$

Difference $=65-(-160)=225$

Difference $=5 \mathrm{k}+200\,\,\,\,\,\,\,…[Given]$

$\Rightarrow 225=5 \mathrm{k}+200$

$\Rightarrow 25=5 \mathrm{k}$

$\Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=5$

content: The solution set for minimizing the function $\mathrm{z}=x+y$ with constraints $x+y \geqslant 2, x+2 y \leqslant 8, y \leqslant 3, x, y \geqslant 0$ contains
explanation:
The corner point of the feasible region are.

A $(2,0)$, B $(8,0), C(2,3), D(0,3)$ and $E(0,2)$

At B $(8,0), \mathrm{z}=8+0=8$

At C $(2,3), z=2+3=5$

At $\mathrm{D}(0,3), \mathrm{z}=0+3=3$

At $\mathrm{E}(0,2) \mathrm{z}=0+2=2$

At $\mathrm{A}(2,0), 2+0=2$

$\therefore \quad \mathrm{Z}$ has minimum value at $\mathrm{E}(0,2)$ and $(2,0)$

$\therefore \quad \mathrm{Z}$ has infinite solution on seg AE .

content: The L.P.P. , minimize $z=30 x+20 y, x+y \leq 8$, $x+2 y \geq 4,6 x+4 y \geq 12, x \geqslant 0, y \geqslant 0$ has
explanation:
The feasible region lies on the origin side of $x+y=8$, on the non-origin side of $6 x+4 y=12$ and $x+2 y=4$, and it is in the first quadrant. The corner points of the feasible region are

$$ \mathrm{A}(8,0), \mathrm{B}(0,8), \mathrm{F}(0,3), \mathrm{G}\left(1, \frac{3}{2}\right) \text { and } \mathrm{C}(4,0) $$

At A $(8,0), z=30(8)+20(0)=240$

At $\mathrm{B}(0,8), \mathrm{z}=3(0)+20(8)=160$

At $\mathrm{F}(0,3), \mathrm{z}=30(0)+20(3)=60$

At $\mathrm{G}(1,3 / 2), \mathrm{z}=30(1)+20(3 / 2)=30+30=60$

At C $(4,0), \mathrm{z}=30(4)+20(0)=120$

$\therefore \quad \mathrm{z}$ has minimum value at $\mathrm{F}(0,3)$ and $\mathrm{G}\left(1, \frac{3}{2}\right)$.

$\therefore \quad z$ has infinite solutions on seg FG.

content: A scholarship amount is given by $\mathrm{z}=550 x+300 y$ and is to be distributed among $x$ boys and $y$ girls. From the graph given below the maximum amount of scholarship is __________
explanation:
At $\mathrm{A}(5,5), \mathrm{z}=550(5)+300(5)=4250$

At $\mathrm{B}(5,10), \mathrm{z}=550(5)+300(10)=5750$

At $\mathrm{C}(11,4), \mathrm{z}=550(11)+300(4)=7250$

At $\mathrm{D}(6,4), \mathrm{z}=550(6)+300(4)=4500$

$\therefore \quad$ The maximum amount of scholarship is 7250 at the point $\mathrm{C}(11,4)$.

content: The feasible region for the constraints $x-2 \leqslant y, x \geqslant y-1, x \geqslant 2, y \leqslant 4, x, y \geqslant 0$, is _________
explanation:
The feasible region lies on the origin side of the lines $x-2=y, x=y-1, y=4$, and on the nonorigin side of the line $x=2$, in the first quadrant.

$\therefore \quad$ Option (B) is the correct answer.

explanation: Feasible region lies on non-origin sides of $2 x+3 y \geq 12$ and $y \geq 3$ and on origin side of $-x+y \leq 3$ and $x \leq 4$ in $1^{\text {st }}$ quadrant.

content: The shaded area in the given figure is a solution set for some system of inequalities. The maximum value of the function $\mathrm{z}=4 x+3 y$ subject to linear constraints given by the system is
explanation:
The corner points of the feasible region are $\mathrm{O}(0,0), \mathrm{A}(6,0), \mathrm{B}(6,4), \mathrm{C}(3,7)$ and $\mathrm{D}(0,5)$.

$z=4 x+3 y$

At $\mathrm{O}(0,0), \mathrm{z}=4(0)+3(0)=0$

At $\mathrm{A}(6,0), \mathrm{z}=4(6)+3(0)=24$

At $\mathrm{B(6,4), z=4(6)+3(4)=36}$

At $\mathrm{C(3,7), z=4(3)+3(7)=33}$

At $\mathrm{D(0,5), z=4(0)+3(5)=15}$

$\therefore \quad$ Maximum value of $z$ is 36.

content: Maximum value of $Z=100 x+70 y$ Subject to $2 x \geq 4, y \leq 3, x+y \leq 8, x, y \geq 0$ is
explanation:
The feasible region lies on the origin side of $y=3$ and $x+y=8$, and on non-origin side of $2 x=4$, in the first quadrant.

The corner points of the feasible region are $\mathrm{A}(2,0), \mathrm{B}(8,0), \mathrm{C}(5,3)$ and $\mathrm{D}(2,3)$.

$\mathrm{Z}=100 x+70 y$

At A(2, 0), Z=200

At B( 8,0$), Z=800$

At $C(5,3), Z=710$

At $D(2,3), Z=410$

$\therefore \quad$ Maximum value of Z is 800 .

explanation:

content: A production unit makes special type of metal chips by combining copper and brass. The standard weight of the chip must be at least 5 gms. The basic ingredients i.e. copper and brass cost ₹8 and ₹ 5 per gm. The durability considerations dictate that the metal chip must no contain more than 4 gms of brass and should contain minimum 2 gms of copper. Then the minimum cost of the metal chip satisfying the above conditions is
explanation:
Let $x$ and $y$ denote the quantity of the basic ingredients of copper and brass respectively.

$\therefore \quad$ cost, $z=8 x+5 y$

Constraints are,

$$\begin{aligned} & x+y \geq 5 \\ & x \geq 2 \\ & y \leq 4 \\ & x \geq 0, y \geq 0 \end{aligned}$$

$\therefore \quad$ Feasible region is as shown in the figure.

$$\begin{aligned} &\therefore \quad \text { Corner points of the feasible region are }\\ &\begin{array}{ll} & \mathrm{A}(2,4), \mathrm{B}(2,3) \text { and } \mathrm{C}(5,0) \\ \therefore \quad & \mathrm{z} \text { at } \mathrm{A}=36, \\ &\mathrm{z} \text { at } \mathrm{B}=31, \\ & \mathrm{z} \text { at } \mathrm{C}=40 \end{array} \end{aligned}$$

$\therefore\quad$ Minimum cost is Rs.31.

explanation:

At $\mathrm{D}(0,3), \mathrm{z}=6$

$\therefore\quad$ Maximum value of $z$ is 20.

explanation:

content: The shaded area in the given figure is a solution set for some system of inequations. The maximum value of the function $$z=10 x+25 y$$ subject to the linear constraints given by the system is
explanation:
We have, $$z=10 x+25 y$$

The shaded area in the given figure is a solution system set for some system of inequations.

The constraints are $$x \leq 3, y \leq 3, x+y \leq 5, x \geq 0$$ and $$y \geq 0$$.

The vertices of the feasible region are $$(0,0),(0,3), (2,3),(3,2)$$ and $$(3,0)$$

At $$(0,0), \quad z=10 \times 0+25 \times 0=0$$

At $$A(0,3), \quad z=10(0)+25(3)=75$$

At $$B(2,3), \quad z=10(2)+25(3)=95$$

At $$C(3,2), \quad z=10(3)+25(2)=80$$

At $$D(3,0), \quad z=10(3)+25(0)=30$$

$$\therefore$$ maximum value of $$z=95$$, where $$x=2$$ and $$y=3$$.

Shaded region lies on non-origin side of $$2 x+y=2$$ and on origin side of the lines $$x-y=1$$ and $$x+2 y=8$$

$$\therefore \quad 2 x+y \geq 2, x-y \leq 1, x+2 y \leq 8$$

content: The shaded area in the figure given below is a solution set of a system of inequations. The minimum value of objective function $$3 x+5 y$$, subject to the linear constraints given by this system of inequations is
explanation:
Let the corner points of the feasible region be A, B, C, D.

Solving equations $$y=3$$ and $$2 x+3 y=12$$, we get

$$\mathrm{A}=(1.5,3)$$

Similarly,

$$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{B}=(4,3) \\\\ & \mathrm{C}=(4,7) \\\\ & \mathrm{D}=\left(\frac{3}{5}, \frac{18}{5}\right) \end{aligned}$$

Let $$Z=3 x+5 y$$

$$\therefore $$ Value of $$Z$$ at point $$A=19.5$$

Value of $$Z$$ at point $$B=27$$

Value of $$Z$$ at point $$C=47$$

Value of $$Z$$ at point $$D=\frac{99}{5}$$

$$\therefore $$ The minimum value of $$Z$$ is 19.5.

content: The vertices of the feasible region for the constraints $$x+y \leq 4, x \leq 2, y \leq 1, x+y \geq 1, x, y \geq 0$$ are
explanation:
Feasible region lies on origin side of $$x+y=4, x=2, y=1$$ and non-origin side of $$x+y=1$$ and in first quadrant.

$$\therefore $$ Vertices of feasible region are $$A(1,0), B(2,0), C(2,1), D(0,1)$$

content: The graphical solution set for the system of inequations $$x-2 y \leq 2,5 x+2 y \geq 10,4 x+5 y \leq 20, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$$ is given by
explanation: Feasible region lies on origin side of $$x-2 y=2, 4 x+5 y=20$$ and on non-origin side of $$5 x+2 y=10$$, in $$1^{\text {st }}$$ quadrant.

explanation:
The shaded region lies:

on non-origin side of line $$3 x+4 y=12$$ i.e., $$3 x+4 y \geq 12$$,

on the side of the line $$y-x=0$$, where $$y \geq x$$ i.e., $$y-x \geq 0$$,

on origin side of line $$y=3$$ i.e., $$y \leq 3$$,

and in first quadrant i.e., $$x \geq 0, y \geq 0$$.

content: Maximum value of $$Z=5 x+2 y$$, subject to $$2 x-y \geq 2, x+2 y \leq 8$$ and $$x, y \geq 0$$ is
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & Z=5 x+2 y \\ & 2 x-y \geq 2, x+2 y \leq 8, x, y \geq 0 \\ & L_1: 2 x-y=2, L_2: x+2 y=8 \\ & \therefore A(1,0), B(0,-2) \text { lie on } L_1 \\ & \quad C(8,0), D(0,4) \text { lie on } L_2 \\ & L_1 \cap L_2 \equiv\left(\frac{12}{5}, \frac{14}{5}\right) \equiv E \end{aligned}$$

$$\therefore$$ Critical points are $$A(1,0), C(8,0), E \equiv\left(\frac{12}{5}, \frac{14}{5}\right)$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore Z_A & =5(1)+2(0)=5 \\ Z_C & =5(8)+2(0)=40 \\ Z_E & =5\left(\frac{12}{5}\right)+2\left(\frac{14}{5}\right)=\frac{88}{5}=17.6 \end{aligned}$$

$$\therefore Z_{\text {max }}=40$$

content: The minimum value of the objective function $$z=4 x+6 y$$ subject to $$x+2 y \geq 80,3 x+y \geq 75, x, y \geq 0$$ is
explanation:
Refer figure.

Required area is shaded.

Vertices of feasible region are $$A=(80,0) ; C=(0,75)$$ and point of intersection of given lines is $$B=(14,33)$$

We have to minimize objective function $$z=4 x+6 y$$

$$\begin{array}{rll} \therefore & z_{(A)} \quad=4(80)+6(0)=320 \\ & z_{(B)} \quad=4(14)+6(33)=254 \\ & z_{(C)} \quad=4(0)+6(75)=450 \end{array}$$

content: The maximum value of the objective function $$z=2 x+3 y$$ subject to the constraints $$x+y \leq 5,2 x+y \geq 4$$ and $$x \geq 0, y \geq 0$$ is
explanation:
Refer figure

Required part is shaded.

We have $$\mathrm{A}=(0,4)$$;

$$\begin{aligned} & B=(2,0) ; C=(5,0) ; \\ & D=(0,5) \end{aligned}$$

We have to maximize fuction

$$\begin{aligned} & z=2 x+3 y \\ & \therefore \quad z_A=2(0)+3(4)=12 \\ & z_B=2(2)+3(0)=4 \\ & z_C=2(5)+3(0)=10 \\ & z_D=2(0)+3(5)=15 \end{aligned}$$

content: The maximum value of $$z=10 x+25 y$$ subject to $$0 \leq x \leq 3,0 \leq y \leq 3, x+y \leq 5$$ occurs at the point.
explanation:
Required area is shaded.

Vertices of the required region are $$0(0,0); \mathrm{A}(3,0) ; \mathrm{B}(3,2) ; \mathrm{C}(2,3) ; \mathrm{D}(0,3)$$

We have to maximize objective function

$$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{z}=10 \mathrm{x}+25 \mathrm{y} & \\ \therefore \quad \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{O})} & =0+0=0 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{A})} & =30+0=30 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{B})} & =30+50=80 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{C})} & =20+75=95 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{D})} & =0+75=75 \end{aligned}$$

content: The objective function $$z=4 x+5 y$$ subjective to $$2 x+y \geq 7 ; 2 x+3 y \leq 15 ; y \leq 3, x \geq 0 ; y \geq 0$$ has minimum value at the point.
explanation:
We have lines $$2 x+y=7,2 x+3 y=15, y=3$$ Refer figure

The required region is shaded.

We have $$A \equiv\left(\frac{7}{2}, 0\right), B \equiv\left(\frac{15}{2}, 0\right)$$

Point of intersection of $$2 x+y=7$$ and $$ y=3 \text { is } D \equiv(2,3)$$

Point of intersection of $$2 x+3 y=15$$ and $$y=3$$ is $$C \equiv(3,3)$$ We have objective function $$\mathrm{z}=4 \mathrm{x}+5 \mathrm{y}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{A})} & =4\left(\frac{7}{2}\right)+5(0) \quad=14+0=14 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{B})} & =4\left(\frac{15}{2}\right)+5(0)=30+0=30 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{C})} & =4(3)+5(3)=12+15=27 \\ \mathrm{z}_{(\mathrm{D})} & =4(2)+5(3)=8+15=23 \end{aligned}$$

Hence minimum value occurs at point A which lies on X axis.

content: The shaded figure given below is the solution set for the linear inequations. Choose the correct option.

For the shaded region, inequalities are as follows.

$$x \geq 0, y \geq 0,2 x+3 y \geq 3, x-6 y \leq 3,3 x+4 y \leq 18,-7 x+14 y \leq 14$$.

Note:

$$-7 x+14 y=14 \Rightarrow 7 x-14=-14 \text { and } 0>-14$$

content: The solution set for the system of linear inequations $$x+y \geq 1 ; 7 x+9 y \leq 63 ; y \leq 5 ; x \leq 6, x \geq 0$$ and $$y \geq 0$$ is represented graphically in the figure. What is the correct option?
explanation:
Lines are $$x+y=1,7x+9y=63,y=5,x=6$$

The required area is shaded.

content: The shaded part of the given figure indicates the feasible region. Then the constraints are
explanation:
Here equation of line OC is $$y=x$$ i.e. $$x-y=0$$ and equation of line AB is $$x=5$$ i.e. $$x-5=0$$

Equation of line BC is $$y=3$$ i.e. $$y-3=0$$

Hence constraints for the shaded region are $$x, y \geq 0, x-5 \leq 0, x-y \geq 0, y-3 \leq 0$$ i.e. $$x, y \geq 0, x \leq 5, x-y \geq 0, y \leq 3$$

explanation:

content: The minimum value of $$Z=5 x+8 y$$ subject to $$x+y \geq 5,0 \leq x \leq 4, y \geq 2, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$$ is
explanation:

Subject to constrants

$$x+y \geq 5,\begin{array}{ll} 0 \leq x \leq 4, y \geq 2, y, \geq 0 \\ \text { Corner point } & z=5 x+8 y \\ A(3,2) & 15+16=31 \\ B(4,2) & 20+16=36 \\ C(0,5) & 0+40=40 \end{array}$$

$$\therefore$$ Minimum value = 31

content: If $z=a x+b y ; a, b>0$ subject to $x \leq 2, y \leq 2, x+y \geq 3, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$ has minimum value at $(2,1)$ only, then......
explanation:
We have, $z=a x+b y, a, b>0$

Subject to constraints $x \leq 2, y \leq 2, x+y \geq 3$

$$x, y \geq 0$$

On taking given constraints as equation, we get the following graph

Here, $A B C A$ is the required feasible region whose comer points are $A(2,1), B(1,2)$ and $C(2,2)$.

Since, It is given that $z=a x+b y, a, b>0$ has minimum value at $(2,1)$.

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \text { Value of } z \text { at }(2,1) < \text { value of } z \text { at }(1,2) \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2 a+b < a+2 b \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a< b \end{aligned}$$

content: The maximum value of $Z=5 x+4 y$, Subject to $y \leq 2 x, x \leq 2 y, x+y \leq 3, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$ is ........
explanation:
We have, $z=5 x+4 y$

Subject to contraints $y \leq 2 x, x \leq 2 y, x+y \leq 3, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$

On taking given constraints as equations, we get the following graph.

Intersecting point of line $y=2 x$ and $x+y=3$ is $A(1,2)$ and intersecting point of line $x=2 y$ and $x+y=3$ is $B(2,1)$.

Here, $O A B O$ is the required feasible region whose corner points are $O(0,0), A(1,2)$ and $B(21)$

Corner points $z=5x+4y$
$O(0,0)$ $5\times0+4\times0=0$
$A(1,2)$ $5\times1+4\times2=13$
$B(2,1)$ $5\times2+4\times1=14$
(maximum)

content: The maximum value of $z=6 x+8 y$ subject to $x-y \geq 0, x+3 y \leq 12, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$ is $\ldots \ldots$.
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text {We have, } z=6 x+8 y \\ & \text { subject to constrants } x-y \geq 0, x+3 y \leq 12, x \geq 0 \text {, } \\ & y \geq 0 \text {. } \end{aligned}$$

On taking given constraints as equations, we get the following graph

Intersecting point of the line $x-y=0$ and $x+3 y=12$ is $B(3,3)$.

Here, $O A B O$ is the required feasible region

Whose corner points are $O(0,0), A(12,0)$ and $B(0,4)$ Now,

Corner points $Z=6x+8y$
$O(0,0)$ $6\times0+8\times0=0$
$A(12,0)$ $6\times12+8\times0=72$ (maximum)
$B(3,3)$ $6\times3+8\times3=42$

$\therefore$ Maximum value of Z is 72.

explanation:

content: The maximum value of $z=9 x+11 y$ subject to $3 x+2 y \leq 12,2 x+3 y \leq 12, x \geq 0, y \geq 0$ is $\ldots \ldots$.
explanation:
We have, $Z=9 x+11 y$

Subject to $3 x+2 y \leq 12,2 x+3 y \leq 12$ On taking given constraints as equation, we get the following graph.

Intersection point of lines $3 x+2 y=12$ and $2 x+3 y=12$ is $C\left(\frac{12}{5}, \frac{12}{5}\right)$

Here, OACBO is the feasible region whose corner points are $O(0,0), A(4,0), C\left(\frac{12}{5}, \frac{12}{5}\right)$ and $B(0,4)$.

Corner points $Z=9x+11y$
$O(0,0)$ $0+0=0$
$A(4,0)$ $9\times4+11\times0=36$
$B(0,4)$ $9\times0+11\times4=44$
$C\left(\frac{12}{5},\frac{12}{5}\right)$ $9\times\frac{12}{5}+11\times\frac{12}{5}=48$ (maximum)

content: The minimum value of $z=10 x+25 y$ subject to $0 \leq x \leq 3,0 \leq y \leq 3, x+y \geq 5$ is $\ldots$
explanation:
Given, $Z=10 x+25 y$

and subject to $0 \leq x \leq 3,0 \leq y \leq 3, x+y \geq 5$

On taking given constraints as equation, we get the following graph.

Here, intersecting point of lines $x=3$ and $x+y=5$ is $A(3,2)$, intersecting point of lines $y=3$ and $x+y=5$ is $B(2,3)$ and intersecting point of lines, $x=3$ and $y=3$ is $C(3,3)$.

Here, $A B C A$ is the feasible region whose corner points are $A(3,2), B(2,3)$ and $C(3,3)$

Corner points $Z=10x+25y$
$A(3,2)$ $10\times3+25\times2=80$ (minimum)
$B(2,3)$ $10\times2+25\times3=95$
$C(3,3)$ $10\times3+25\times3=105$
